mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS is falling or held near the zero lower bound, equity discount rates stay suppressed and risk appetite improves. That setting supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next month because future cash flows are priced with a lower real funding hurdle.
failure_condition: Reject if 20d forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of periods with FEDFUNDS at or below recent lows, or if the signal's rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in RSAFS after a weak patch signals improving nominal spending and corporate revenue momentum. That improvement should lift earnings expectations and produce positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change turns positive after being negative in the prior 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if positive RSAFS reversals do not coincide with above-baseline 20d SPX returns, or if triggered instances produce negative median forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in UNRATE after a prior rise indicates labor-market stabilization and lower recession risk. That should compress equity risk premia and generate positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE falls by at least 0.2pp over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if 60d SPX returns are not higher after UNRATE declines than after flat or rising UNRATE, or if the effect vanishes in rolling 4-quarter tests.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO expands while CPIAUCSL stays subdued, real activity is improving without an inflation shock. That combination supports multiple expansion and positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Reject if positive 20d SPX returns do not concentrate in episodes where INDPRO rises and CPIAUCSL inflation remains contained, or if either leg alone explains the effect equally well.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in HOUST signals a housing-demand slowdown that feeds into weaker construction activity and broader cyclical demand. That deterioration should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 8% over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if large HOUST declines are followed by positive or neutral 20d SPX returns more often than negative returns, or if the signal loses significance after controlling for prior HOUST level.

mechanism_narrative: When PAYEMS growth slows while UNRATE rises, payroll deterioration and rising slack confirm a weakening labor market. This combination should predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as recession odds rise.
failure_condition: Reject if instances of payroll slowdown plus rising unemployment do not produce materially worse 60d SPX returns than the unconditional sample, or if one proxy fully subsumes the joint effect.

mechanism_narrative: A low FEDFUNDS level combined with subdued CPIAUCSL inflation creates a favorable real-rate environment for equities. The signal is positive only when policy is easy and inflation is not reaccelerating, because then discount-rate relief dominates margin pressure.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS below 1% and CPIAUCSL 3-month inflation below 2%
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show higher 5d SPX returns than untargeted periods, or if the effect remains absent even when both low-rate and low-inflation conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: After a negative GDP print, a rebound in GDPC1 signals recession risk is being repriced lower and future aggregate demand is recovering. That should translate into positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth turns positive after a prior negative quarter
failure_condition: Reject if post-contraction GDP rebounds fail to precede positive 60d SPX performance, or if the signal is not stronger than a simple unconditional mean-reversion benchmark.
